figures.caption: FIG. 3. Ideal (red solid line) and non-ideal (blue dashed line) radial normal components of the pressure tensor (top panel) and the opposite of its derivative (bottom panel).
TABLE II. Representation of the IKC for a given pair of particles (i, j) and a spherical shell of radius r – Cases 1 and 2. Case Schematic Representation

FIG. 11. Normal component of pressure tensors in the radial direction for helium bubbles of NHe = 512 atoms in 24 different lead–lithium alloys (BEAM model [36]). Kinetic (pK), virial (pV ) and total (pN = pK + pV ) profiles are depicted using blue, orange and green circles, respectively. In addition, pidN have been estimated by fitting the corresponding pN values, and drawn using green dashed lines.
TABLE III. Representation of the IKC for a given pair of particles (i, j) and a spherical shell of radius r – Case 3 and 4. Case Schematic Representation

FIG. 8. Interfacial tension (top), interfacial radii (middle), and interfacial free energy variations (bottom), as function of the solvent composition (xPb = 1 − xLi). All cases consider He/Pb–Li spherical interfaces, where all helium atoms segregated into a single spherical cluster of NHe = 512. Blue circles and orange (empty) diamonds indicate that the LM phase has been modelled with the Al-Awad’s[29] or Belashchenko’s[36] (respectively), combined with the same potentials for helium atoms (TTS for He–He and Li–He [31, 75], SBMILL for Pb– He).
TABLE I. Summary of IKC cases for planar symmetries.
TABLE IV. Representation of the IKC for a given pair of particles (i, j) and a spherical shell of radius r – Case 5. Case Schematic Representation

FIG. 10. Normal component of pressure tensors in the radial direction for helium bubbles of NHe = 512 atoms in 24 different lead–lithium alloys (AEAM model [29]). Kinetic (pK), virial (pV ) and total (pN = pK + pV ) profiles are depicted using blue, orange and green circles, respectively. In addition, pidN have been estimated by fitting the corresponding pN values, and drawn using green dashed lines.
FIG. 9. Interfacial tension values for varying helium cluster sizes at constant temperature (1021 K). Simulations have been performed using the Al-Awad force field (2023) [29].

FIG. 7. Interfacial tension of a planar surface as function of the solvent atomic fraction (composition) along the 1021 K isotherm. Blue and orange circles correspond to IT values where lead–lithium alloys were modelled by AEAM-2023[29] and BEAM-2019 [36] potentials, respectively. Both cases consider TTS[31] and SBMILL[30] potentials for helium interactions. Dashed lines indicate ideal-mixture predictions.
